title: Understand: The Road to the Civil War
description: Check your understanding of the events leading up to the Civil War with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: America’s Second Founding: Slavery, the Civil War, Emancipation, and Reconstruction
The Road to the Civil War
Understand: The Road to the Civil War

assessment_items.question_content:
**Ideas about freedom, equality, and the Constitution that emerged in the anti-slavery movement became the foundation for the rise of Abraham Lincoln and the birth of which political party?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Federalist Party
Republican Party
Whig Party
Democratic Party

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which TWO events led to the Compromise of 1820?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The purchase of the Louisiana Territory from France
The request for Missouri to become a state
The end of the American Revolution
The secession of the southern states

assessment_items.question_content:
**What were the terms of the Missouri Compromise of 1820? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Missouri was admitted as a slave state to the United States.
Any state above the 36°30′ line would be a free state.
Any state below the 36°30′ line must be a slave state.
No further territories would be admitted to the union.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the impact of the Compromise of 1850?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It allowed states formed in new territories to decide for themselves whether they would be free states or slave states.
It solved the issue of the expansion of slavery by banning slavery in any territories west of the Mississippi River.
It stated the terms for secession for states that wanted to leave the Union.
It established that all Black people, regardless of their status as free or enslaved, were not citizens of the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What major act of Congress did the Supreme Court declare unconstitutional in *Dred Scott v. Sandford*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Missouri Compromise of 1820
The Compromise of 1850
The Kansas-Nebraska Act
The Louisiana Purchase

assessment_items.question_content:
**What previous act of Congress did the Kansas-Nebraska Act repeal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Missouri Compromise of 1820
The Compromise of 1850
The Emancipation Proclamation
The Louisiana Purchase

assessment_items.question_content:
The Compromise of 1850 sought to settle disputes between North and South after the United States acquired new territories in the Mexican-American War. It admitted California into the Union as a free state but also included a strict Fugitive Slave Law. 

**What was the effect of this compromise?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Angry reactions in both the North and the South led to even more division in the United States, pushing the country toward civil war.
Southern slaveholders were outraged about the Compromise, while people in the North did not object to it.
The Compromise eased tensions between the North and South and led to a brief period of harmony before the Kansas-Nebraska Act was passed.
The southern states seceded from the United States, as they found the Compromise problematic.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What part of the Constitution did southern states use to justify their secession from the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Article V
Article VII
The Fourth Amendment
The Tenth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was Lincoln’s argument about the constitutionality of Southern secession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It was unconstitutional because there was no secession clause in the Constitution and the Union was intended to be perpetual.
It was unconstitutional because the South did not form a new federal government to replace Congress and the president.
It was constitutional because it wasn’t explicitly forbidden in the text of the U.S. Constitution.
It was constitutional because it was the will of the people, and the Constitution prioritizes popular sovereignty.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the effect of Southern secession from the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The start of the Civil War
The passage of the Kansas-Nebraska Act
The implementation of the Compromise of 1850
The election of Abraham Lincoln to the presidency

assessment_items.question_content:
**By repealing the Missouri Compromise of 1820, which drew the horizontal line between slave and free states across the West along the 36°30′ parallel, the Kansas-Nebraska Act did which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Reopened the possibility of slavery in new territories north of the Missouri Compromise line
Required all states to a vote on the legality of slavery, causing Northern abolitionists to fear that Massachusetts, Ohio, and Pennsylvania could become slave states
Made slavery illegal in Kansas and Nebraska, angering White Southerners who believed that they would have been slave states
Overturned the terms of the Compromise of 1850 by extending slavery to territories west of the Mississippi River

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was “popular sovereignty” in the context of the Kansas-Nebraska Act?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Citizens living in each territory could decide whether that territory would enter the Union as a slave state or a free state.
Citizens of each territory could negotiate their own treaties with Native Americans.
Citizens of new territories could decide whether they wanted to join the Union or become independent nations.
Representatives for each of the new territories could vote on whether they wished to secede from the Union.
